description: Evaluates authentication service maturity—authentication systems, IAM ownership, KPIs/KRIs, modern MFA and passwordless authentication, phishing-resistant methods, risk-based adaptive authentication, non-human and API authentication, and SOC logging with anomaly detection.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: An authentication service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: Authentication system
capabilities.description: A formal authentication system authenticates subjects to network and applications enterprise-wide, supports cloud identity providers and federation, is reviewed regularly, and integrates with access control decision and enforcement points.
evaluation_focus: Formal authentication system for network and applications
Cloud identity provider and federation support
Integration with access control decision and enforcement points

capabilities.name: Authentication service owner
capabilities.description: An IAM engineer or dedicated authentication owner configures authentication policies, with central management when authentication is siloed across locations.
evaluation_focus: IAM engineer or authentication owner for policy configuration

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Authentication KPIs and KRIs—including MFA coverage, failed authentication rate, and passkey adoption—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: MFA and phishing-resistant authentication coverage metrics
Failed authentication and credential attack metrics

capabilities.name: Modern and phishing-resistant authentication
capabilities.description: MFA, passwordless (FIDO2/passkeys), and phishing-resistant authentication are documented, deployed enterprise-wide or in scoped areas, and reviewed for enhancement.
evaluation_focus: Enterprise-wide vs scoped phishing-resistant MFA deployment
Regular MFA and passwordless configuration review

capabilities.name: Risk-based and adaptive authentication
capabilities.description: Authentication decisions adapt to risk signals such as device trust, geolocation, behavior anomalies, and step-up requirements for high-risk access.
evaluation_focus: Adaptive step-up authentication for high-risk sessions
Integration of device and behavioral signals into auth decisions

capabilities.name: Non-human and API authentication
capabilities.description: Service accounts, workloads, APIs, and machine-to-machine clients authenticate using governed methods such as OAuth, client credentials, and certificate-based auth—not shared human passwords.
evaluation_focus: Governed service account and workload authentication
OAuth and API client credential authentication standards
Prohibition of shared credentials for non-human identities

capabilities.name: Authentication logging and anomaly detection
capabilities.description: Authentication events including lifecycle changes and failed authentications are logged securely to the SOC, with near-real-time detection of anomalous auth patterns.
evaluation_focus: Secure authentication event logging to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
